evals.prompt: Our Go CI job started failing after a toolchain update and I need to figure out whether GOROOT or GOTOOLDIR is stale.
evals.expected_behavior: Captures go version and go env, checks for stale GOROOT/GOTOOLDIR overrides, and separates environment issues from package code defects before editing.
assertions: Inspects go version and go env (GOROOT, GOTOOLDIR, GOPATH) before code changes
Considers stale shell overrides as a first-class cause
Re-runs package-scoped go build/test after the fix

evals.prompt: go test passes on macOS but fails on Linux CI with a TempDir cleanup error while a background event still writes.
evals.expected_behavior: Treats this as CI-parity/test-isolation, looks for t.TempDir races or late finished-event writes, and proposes the smallest isolation fix.
assertions: Frames the issue as CI/OS parity or test isolation rather than pure product logic
Mentions TempDir cleanup, background writes, or waiting for finished events
Avoids jumping to go-error-patterns style signature redesign

evals.prompt: Why doesn't func(string) *ValidationError satisfy func(string) error in Go, and what should I pass instead?
evals.expected_behavior: Routes to go-error-patterns because this is error-type design, not a build/toolchain failure.
assertions: Does not treat this primarily as a GOROOT or CI-parity problem
Points toward go-error-patterns or constructor-wrapping guidance
